tasks.title: Create scaffold template files
tasks.body: Create the following template files under internal/project/templates/scaffold/: gitignore.tmpl (content from the inline string at scaffold_service.go:190-201), readme-root.md.tmpl, readme-system.md.tmpl, readme-base-prompts.md.tmpl, readme-docs.md.tmpl, readme-archive.md.tmpl (content from the scaffoldREADMEs map at scaffold_service.go:25-128). These are static templates with no Go template variables. Content must be byte-for-byte identical to the current inline strings.
acceptance_criteria: Each template file contains the exact content currently in the Go string literals
Templates are embedded via the existing go:embed all:templates directive

tasks.title: Migrate scaffold_service.go to use templates
tasks.body: Replace the scaffoldREADMEs map and inline gitignore string in scaffold_service.go with calls to RenderToFile (or ResolveTemplate + WriteBase for scaffold paths that go through the tier system). The ScaffoldService already has access to PromptRepository via its prompts field. Remove the scaffoldREADMEs map variable and the gitignore string literal. Ensure the scaffold Init and Reinit flows produce identical directory structures and file contents.
acceptance_criteria: scaffoldREADMEs map is removed from scaffold_service.go
Inline gitignore string literal is removed
Generated files are byte-for-byte identical to previous output

tasks.title: Snapshot tests for scaffold templates
tasks.body: Write snapshot tests that capture the output of each scaffold-generated file (gitignore and all five READMEs) and assert byte-for-byte identity with the expected content. Use Go test fixtures or golden files. These tests verify the migration introduced no regressions. Test both the Init and Reinit paths to confirm templates are correctly resolved from the tier system.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot tests cover gitignore and all five READMEs

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Scaffold Templates is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Created 6 scaffold template files (gitignore.tmpl + 5 readme templates) under internal/project/templates/scaffold/. Content verified byte-for-byte identical to Go string literals in scaffold_service.go.

Migrated scaffold_service.go: removed scaffoldREADMEs map (100+ lines) and inline gitignore string, replaced with scaffoldFiles mapping that reads from embedded templates under templates/scaffold/. Net reduction of ~97 lines.

Added snapshot tests for all 6 scaffold files (gitignore + 5 READMEs). Init path verifies byte-for-byte match with embedded templates. Reinit path verifies 5 files outside base tier survive unchanged, and separately confirms base-prompts README is lost during base-tier teardown. Recorded gap for the lost README.

gaps.description: Reinit does not rewrite system/base/prompts/README.md after tearing down the base tier. The scaffold file is written by Init's writeScaffoldFiles() but Reinit only rebuilds config and prompts, leaving the README absent after reinit.

task-0001.objective: Extract scaffold string literals into embedded template files
task-0001.what_happened: Created six .tmpl files under internal/project/templates/scaffold/: gitignore.tmpl from the inline string at scaffold_service.go:190-201, and five readme templates (root, system, base-prompts, docs, archive) from the scaffoldREADMEs map at scaffold_service.go:25-128. Resolved Go string concatenation for backtick-quoted inline code in readme-system and readme-base-prompts. Verified byte-for-byte match with a standalone Go comparison program.
went_well: The existing go:embed all:templates directive picked up the new scaffold/ subdirectory with zero changes to embedded.go
Byte-for-byte verification caught potential issues early, confirming the Go string concatenation was resolved correctly in the template files
improvements: The template files are currently unused; the next task needs to wire scaffold_service.go to load from them instead of the inline literals
action_items: Next task should modify scaffold_service.go to read these templates via the embed.FS instead of using the hardcoded strings

task-0002.objective: Replace inline string literals in scaffold_service.go with reads from embedded template files
task-0002.what_happened: Removed the 100-line scaffoldREADMEs map and inline gitignore string. Replaced them with a scaffoldFiles mapping (template path -> output path) and a writeScaffoldFiles() method that reads from the embed.FS. The Init method now calls writeScaffoldFiles() instead of manually writing each file. Updated the test in scaffold_readme_test.go to verify against embedded template content rather than the deleted map.
went_well: The change was clean: -137 lines, +40 lines. All existing tests pass without modification beyond the one test that directly referenced the scaffoldREADMEs variable
The error path tests (GitignoreWriteFailure) still work because writeScaffoldFiles produces the same error message format
improvements: The scaffold files are written in map iteration order, which is random. Not a correctness issue since each file is independent, but could matter for debugging if a write failure depends on prior files existing
action_items: None: deliverable is complete and all acceptance criteria are met

task-0003.objective: Write snapshot tests verifying byte-for-byte identity of scaffold-generated files across Init and Reinit paths
task-0003.what_happened: Added three tests to scaffold_service_test.go: Init_SnapshotScaffoldFiles (all 6 files), Reinit_SnapshotScaffoldFiles (5 files outside base tier), and Reinit_BaseTierREADMELost. The Reinit test revealed that system/base/prompts/README.md is destroyed during Reinit's base-tier teardown and never rewritten. Recorded this as gap-scaffold-templates-1.
went_well: Subtests per file give granular failure output: each scaffold file runs as its own t.Run case
The Reinit snapshot test caught a real behavioral gap: the base-prompts README is silently lost
improvements: The snapshot comparison uses string equality which gives poor diff output on large files. A line-by-line diff helper would make failures easier to diagnose
action_items: Fix gap-scaffold-templates-1: Reinit should call writeScaffoldFiles() (or at minimum rewrite the base-prompts README) after rebuilding the base tier
